item_text: real-time communication service -> models — real-time communication service
best_testcase_text: test case id: tc-chat-001 title: send message success module: chat source pages: 8 preconditions: two users are logged in and connected. test data: message="hello" test steps: 1. user1 enters a message in the chat input field. 2. user1 clicks the send button. 3. app1 sends the message to app2. 4. app2 sends the notification to server. 5. server retrieves the message from the database. 6. server sends the message to user2. 7. user2 receives the message. expected result: message is successfully sent and received by both users.

item_text: if the user is logged in, then they can create an order
best_testcase_text: test case id: tc-ordermanagement-001 title: create order success module: order management source pages: 2 preconditions: user is logged in and has added products to the cart. test data: userid=1, productid=1, quantity=2 test steps: 1. user navigates to the order creation page. 2. user selects products from the cart. 3. user enters the quantity for each selected product. 4. app sends order details to the server. 5. server validates the order details. 6. server saves the order in the database. expected result: order is successfully created and saved in the database.

item_text: if the user has a product in their cart, then they can add it to their order
best_testcase_text: test case id: tc-ordermanagement-001 title: create order success module: order management source pages: 2 preconditions: user is logged in and has added products to the cart. test data: userid=1, productid=1, quantity=2 test steps: 1. user navigates to the order creation page. 2. user selects products from the cart. 3. user enters the quantity for each selected product. 4. app sends order details to the server. 5. server validates the order details. 6. server saves the order in the database. expected result: order is successfully created and saved in the database.

item_text: if the user has a product in their cart, then they can remove it from their cart
best_testcase_text: test case id: tc-productmanagement-001 title: create product success module: product management source pages: 2 preconditions: user is logged in. test data: name="product1", price=10.99, description="description1" test steps: 1. user navigates to the product creation page. 2. user enters product details. 3. app sends product details to the server. 4. server validates the product details. 5. server saves the product in the database. expected result: product is successfully created and saved in the database.
